hypothesis: Introducing a 5–10% cross-modality contrastive alignment loss that pulls together co-representations of the same system from SMILES/SELFIES, 3D coordinates, and short natural-language descriptions during pretraining will, at fixed compute, significantly reduce the OOD–ID CKNNA inflation and increase OOD property-prediction accuracy on OMol25 for models trained primarily on OMat24/sAlex compared to otherwise-identical models without this loss.
